name: Alertmanager
description: Bring Prometheus Alertmanager alerts into Mattermost with the runbook attached. Each alert post includes the runbook's first three diagnostic commands inline, pre-filled with the failing host and pod from the alert's labels — copy-paste ready at 3am. Run `/alertmanager help` for the full command list.

settings_schema.header:
## Alertmanager Plugin

Prometheus Alertmanager alerts in Mattermost, with the runbook attached.

- 📚 [Documentation](/plugins/com.mattermost.alertmanager/public/help/home.html)
- 📖 [Runbook library](/plugins/com.mattermost.alertmanager/public/runbooks/home.html)
- 📈 [Sample Prometheus rules](/plugins/com.mattermost.alertmanager/public/help/prometheus-rules.html)
- 📊 [Org-wide inventory (sysadmin)](/plugins/com.mattermost.alertmanager/admin/inventory)

Run `/alertmanager help` in any channel for commands. Version `__PLUGIN_VERSION__` — [release notes](__PLUGIN_RELEASE_URL__).
settings_schema.footer:
### Most-used commands

| Command | When |
|---|---|
| `/alertmanager add <team> <channel> <am-url>` | First-time setup — creates the 30 canonical receivers in a channel |
| `/alertmanager rules` | Get the sample Prometheus rules (view or download) |
| `/alertmanager list` | See receivers bound to this channel |

Run `/alertmanager help` in chat for the full command list.

settings.help_text: Hours to keep the DM'd receivers.yml/routes.yml (they contain webhook tokens) before auto-deleting; 0 disables auto-delete.
Optional PEM CA bundle so the plugin trusts self-signed or internal Alertmanager certs on its outbound calls; empty uses the system CAs.
Optional bearer token that turns on the Prometheus metrics endpoint at `/plugins/com.mattermost.alertmanager/metrics`; empty disables it (404).
Optional host:port for the `api_url` written into alertmanager.yml, for when Alertmanager reaches Mattermost on a different network path than users (see placeholder); empty uses the SiteURL.
Days before the plugin DMs sysadmins to rotate a receiver's webhook (reminder only — no auto-rotation); 0 disables, 90 is a good baseline.
